children.heading: Disclosures for new mortgages at time of transaction
children.label: a Disclosures for new mortgages at time of transaction
i
children.text: a written initial amortization schedule; and

children.label: I
children.text: that the mortgagor may cancel the requirement in accordance with indicating the date on which the mortgagor may request cancellation, based solely on the initial amortization schedule;section 4902(a) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: that the mortgagor may request cancellation in accordance with earlier than provided for in the initial amortization schedule, based on actual payments;section 4902(a) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: that the requirement for private mortgage insurance will automatically terminate on the termination date in accordance with , and what that termination date is with respect to that mortgage; andsection 4902(b) of this title

children.label: IV
children.text: that there are exemptions to the right to cancellation and automatic termination of a requirement for private mortgage insurance in accordance with , and whether such an exemption applies at that time to that transaction; andsection 4902(g) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the mortgagor may cancel the requirement in accordance with on the cancellation date, and that the servicer will notify the mortgagor when the cancellation date is reached;section 4902(a) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the requirement for private mortgage insurance will automatically terminate on the termination date, and that on the termination date, the mortgagor will be notified of the termination or that the requirement will be terminated as soon as the mortgagor is current on loan payments; and

children.label: iii
children.text: there are exemptions to the right of cancellation and automatic termination of a requirement for private mortgage insurance in accordance with , and whether such an exemption applies at that time to that transaction.section 4902(g) of this title

children.heading: Disclosures for excepted transactions
children.label: 2 Disclosures for excepted transactions
children.text: In the case of a residential mortgage transaction described in , at the time at which the transaction is consummated, the mortgagee shall provide written notice to the mortgagor that in no case may private mortgage insurance be required beyond the date that is the midpoint of the amortization period of the loan, if the mortgagor is current on payments required by the terms of the residential mortgage.section 4902(g)(1) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the rights of the mortgagor under this chapter to cancellation or termination of the private mortgage insurance requirement; and

children.label: B
children.text: an address and telephone number that the mortgagor may use to contact the servicer to determine whether the mortgagor may cancel the private mortgage insurance.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: 4 Applicability
children.text: Paragraphs (1) through (3) shall apply with respect to each residential mortgage transaction consummated on or after the date that is 1 year after .July 29, 1998
that the private mortgage insurance may, under certain circumstances, be canceled by the mortgagor (with the consent of the mortgagee or in accordance with applicable State law); and
an address and telephone number that the mortgagor may use to contact the servicer to determine whether the mortgagor may cancel the private mortgage insurance.

children.heading: Inclusion in other annual notices
children.label: c Inclusion in other annual notices
children.text: The information and disclosures required under subsection (b) and subsection (a)(3) may be provided on the annual disclosure relating to the escrow account made as required under the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974 [ et seq.], or as part of the annual disclosure of interest payments made pursuant to Internal Revenue Service regulations, and on a form promulgated by the Internal Revenue Service for that purpose.12 U.S.C. 2601

children.heading: Standardized forms
children.label: d Standardized forms
children.text: The mortgagee or servicer may use standardized forms for the provision of disclosures required under this section, which disclosures shall relate to the mortgagor’s rights under this chapter.
